title: Always-worktree in run.go and remove mode flags
description:
Simplify cmd/tk/cmd/run.go to always create a worktree and remove all mode flags.

Changes:
- Remove flags: --worktree, --pool, --ralph, --swarm, --parallel, --checkpoint-interval, --skip-dep-analysis, --stale-timeout
- Add flags: --skip-wrap-up, --no-merge, --pr
- Remove runWorktree, runParallel, runSwarmMode, runRalphMode, runPoolMode variables
- Remove all mode dispatch logic (if swarm / else if pool / else ralph)
- Remove multi-epic sequential loop (only accept one epic-id argument)
- Remove runParallelEpics, runParallelEpicsWithPool, runEpicWithPool functions
- Remove resolvePoolSize, computeMaxWaveWidth (moved to wave package)

New flow:
1. Parse single epic-id (or --auto)
2. Create worktree (or reuse existing)
3. Run engine (wave loop)
4. Run wrap-up phase (unless --skip-wrap-up)
5. Merge or preserve worktree

Update command help text and usage examples.
acceptance_criteria: tk run accepts single epic-id. Always creates worktree. No mode flags. --skip-wrap-up, --no-merge, --pr flags work. Old flags produce helpful error message.
closed_reason: Simplified run.go to always-worktree, single epic, no mode flags.
